ARM_CFG_GROUPS.BEGIN:
# ARM (Automatic Ripping Machine) config file

#################
## ARM Options ##
#################
ARM_CFG_GROUPS.EMBY_ADDITIONAL: # Parameters to enable automatic library scan in Emby.  This will trigger only if MainFeature is true above.

ARM_NAME:
# A friendly name for this machine. Useful if you are running multiple instances.
# Used in notification titles.
PREVENT_99:
# Workaround for Track 99
# A DRM scheme used on some DVDs which creates fake titles which confuses MakeMKV.
# When set to "false" affected DVDs will be passed to MakeMKV for ripping, but this can crash or hang
# Setting this to true will eject the disc and log only to the system logging
ARM_CHECK_UDF: # Distinguish UDF video discs from UDF data discs.  Requires mounting disc so adds a few seconds to the identify script.
GET_VIDEO_TITLE:
# When enabled if the disc is a DVD use dvdid to calculate a crc64 and query Windows Media Meta Services for the Movie Title.
# For BluRays attempts to extract the title from an XML file on the disc
ARM_API_KEY:
# ARM dvd crc64 api key
# This is only needed if you would like to send movies to the database
DISABLE_LOGIN:
# Do you want to be forced to login to view/edit jobs
# True will leave all of the pages open to be editable/viewable by anyone
# False will require you to login to view/edit jobs or settings from the ui
# ARMui will need to be restared for this to update
VIDEOTYPE:
# Video type identification.  Options are "auto", "series", "movie".
# If "auto" then ARM will get the video type when querying the movie webservice.  This is default.
# If the disc is not clearly a movie or series, or if ARM is having difficulty getting the right video type
# you can override the automatic identification with "series" or "movie"
MINLENGTH: # Minimum length of track for ARM rip (in seconds)
MAXLENGTH:
# Maximum length of track for ARM rip (in seconds)
# Use "99999" to indicate no maximum length
MANUAL_WAIT_TIME: # Wait time for manual identification (in seconds)
DATE_FORMAT:
# Allows you to format the date/time to your own liking
# This will be used throughout ARM and ARMui
ALLOW_DUPLICATES:
## Do you want to allow Rips of the same disk multiple times
## With this set as false the task will exit if it recognises the same movie being ripped
## recommended to set to true for series
MAX_CONCURRENT_MAKEMKVINFO:
# Number of MakeMKV info calls that are allowed to run.
#This can be set to 1 if makemkvcon info calls lead to crashes on backup or mkv calls.
# Set to 0 to disable
DRIVE_READY_TIMEOUT:
# How long (in seconds) to wait for the drive to become ready after disc insertion.
# Some drives take longer to spin up. If the drive reports NO_DISC for the majority
# of this period, ARM exits gracefully instead of throwing an error.
DATA_RIP_PARAMETERS: # Additional parameters for dd. e.g. "conv=noerror,sync" for ignoring read errors
METADATA_PROVIDER:
# This selects the metadata provider, Each provider has their own ups and downs
# But a general rule would be 
# OMDB for movies and shows 
# TMDB for movies only
# You will still need to provide an api key for the provider you have selected
GET_AUDIO_TITLE:
# Set to one of "none", "musicbrainz", "freecddb"
# if "musicbrainz" is used the disc information are asked from musicbrainz.org
# if "none" is used no label is identified
RIP_POSTER:
# Rip DVD Posters from JACKET_P folder
# Requires FFmpeg
AUTO_EJECT:
# Auto-ejects disks
# Auto-ejects disks when complete etc
# Set to false to disable auto-ejection
ABCDE_CONFIG_FILE: # Location of your ABCDE config file
RIP_SPEED_PROFILE:
# CD rip speed profile — controls cdparanoia error-correction.
# "safe" = full paranoia (slowest, best for scratched discs)
# "fast" = disable extra paranoia (good balance, ~2-4x faster)
# "fastest" = no error correction (pristine discs only, ~5-10x faster)
RAW_PATH:
# Path to raw MakeMKV directory
# Destination for MakeMKV output
TRANSCODE_PATH:
# Intermediary directory for transcoding files
# Used by external transcoder service
COMPLETED_PATH:
# Final directory of completed files
# Ripped files end up here
MUSIC_PATH: # Music rip output directory (used by abcde)
EXTRAS_SUB:
# Movie subdirectory name for "extras"
# Valid names are dependent on your media server
# For Emby see https://github.com/MediaBrowser/Wiki/wiki/Movie%20naming#user-content-movie-extras
# For Plex see https://support.plex.tv/hc/en-us/articles/200220677
LOGPATH:
# Path to directory to hold log files
# Make sure to include trailing /
LOGLEVEL:
# Log level.  DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL
# The default is INFO
# If you are experiencing difficulties set this to DEBUG
LOGLIFE:
# How long to let log files live before deleting (in days)
# Set to 0 to disable
WEBSERVER_IP:
# IP address of web server (this machine)
# Use x.x.x.x to autodetect the IP address to use
UI_BASE_URL:
# Base URL to use for notifications and display purposes
#Be sure to include protocol and port if needed (e.g. http://example.com:8091 or https://example.com)
SET_MEDIA_PERMISSIONS:
# Enabling this setting will allow you to adjust the default file permissions of the outputted files
# The default value is set to 777 for read/write/execute for all users, but can be changed below using the "CHMOD_VALUE" setting
# This setting is helpful when storing the data locally on the system
SET_MEDIA_OWNER: # Don't bother. This does not do anything yet
MAKEMKV_PERMA_KEY: # Storage for a purchased key the user may have paid for. Populating will prevent the beta key updater from running.
MAKEMKV_COMMUNITY_KEYDB:
# Download the community keydb.cfg from the FindVUK Online Database at container startup.
# This provides Volume Unique Keys (VUKs) needed for Blu-ray decryption when MakeMKV's
# built-in key server is unavailable. Set to false to disable automatic updates.
RIPMETHOD:
# Method of MakeMKV to use for Blu Ray discs.  Options are "mkv", "backup" or "backup_dvd".
# backup decrypts the dvd and then copies it to the hard drive.
# backup_dvd forces arm to extract the dvd with MakeMKV prior to any post-processing.
# *** NOTE: MakeMKV only supports the backup or backup_dvd method on BluRay discs.
MKV_ARGS:
# MakeMKV Arguments
# MakeMKV Profile used for controlling Audio Track Selection.
# This is the default profile MakeMKV uses for Audio track selection. Updating this file or changing it is considered
# to be advanced usage of MakeMKV. But this will allow users to alternatively tell makemkv to select HD audio tracks and etc.
# MKV_ARGS: "--profile=/opt/arm/default.mmcp.xml"
# MKV_ARGS: "--debug=-stdout"  # this will enable more detailed logging
DELRAWFILES: # Remove any files created in the raw path for the job after processing is complete
MAINFEATURE:
# Rip only the main feature track (largest title).
# When true, MakeMKV will rip only the main feature; extras are skipped.
# When false, all tracks meeting MINLENGTH/MAXLENGTH criteria are ripped.
EMBY_REFRESH: # Scan emby library after successful placement of mainfeature (see above)
EMBY_SERVER:
# Server parameters
# Server can be ip address or domain name
EMBY_CLIENT: # Emby authentication fluff parameters.  These can be anything.
EMBY_USERNAME: # Emby authentication parameters.  These are parameters that must be set to a current user in Emby.
EMBY_USERID:
# EMBY_USERID is the user ID associated with the username above.  You can find this by going to the following address on your emby server
# <server>:<port>/Users/Public and getting the ID value for the username above.
EMBY_PASSWORD:
# This is the SHA1 encrypted password for the username above.  You can generate the SHA1 hash of your password by executing the following at
# the command line: 
# echo -n your-password | sha1sum | awk '{print $1}'
# or using an online generator like the one located at http://www.sha1-online.com/
EMBY_API_KEY:
# Emby API key.  This can be found (generated) by going to Advanced/Security in the 
# Emby dashboard
NOTIFY_JOBID: # Add Job ID to Notification Title
PB_KEY:
# Pushbullet API Key
# Leave empty to disable Pushbullet notifications
IFTTT_KEY:
# IFTTT API KEY
# Leave empty to disable IFTTT notifications
PO_USER_KEY:
# Pushover API User and Application Key
# Leave User key empty to disable Pushover notifications
OMDB_API_KEY:
# OMDB_API_KEY
# omdbapi.com API Key
# See README-OMDBAPI for background and info
# This is the API key for omdbapi.com queries.
# More info at https://omdbapi.com/
TMDB_API_KEY:
# TMDB_API_KEY
# https://www.themoviedb.org/ API Key
# See README-OMDBAPI for background and info
# This is the API key for https://www.themoviedb.org/ queries.
# More info at https://www.themoviedb.org/documentation/api
JSON_URL:
# JSON_URL
# Url to send Notifications to in JSON format
MOVIE_TITLE_PATTERN:
# Pattern for movie display titles.
# Use {variable} placeholders — available variables are listed in the UI.
MOVIE_FOLDER_PATTERN:
# Pattern for movie folder names. Use / for nested directories.
# Use {variable} placeholders — available variables are listed in the UI.
TV_TITLE_PATTERN:
# Pattern for TV series display titles.
# Use {variable} placeholders — available variables are listed in the UI.
TV_FOLDER_PATTERN:
# Pattern for TV series folder names. Use / for nested directories.
# Use {variable} placeholders — available variables are listed in the UI.
MUSIC_TITLE_PATTERN:
# Pattern for music display titles.
# Use {variable} placeholders — available variables are listed in the UI.
MUSIC_FOLDER_PATTERN:
# Pattern for music folder names. Use / for nested directories.
# Use {variable} placeholders — available variables are listed in the UI.
MUSIC_MULTI_DISC_SUBFOLDERS:
# Enable per-disc subfolders for multi-CD album rips.
# When true, multi-disc sets produce: Artist/Album/Disc 1/track.flac, Disc 2/track.flac, etc.
# When false, all discs are mixed into one folder.
MUSIC_DISC_FOLDER_PATTERN:
# Folder name pattern for each disc in a multi-disc set.
# {num} is replaced with the disc number (1, 2, 3…).
# Examples: "Disc {num}", "CD {num}", "CD{num}"
APPRISE:
# File location of your apprise.yaml file. 
# Docker default is '/etc/arm/config/apprise.yaml'.
index_refresh:
How often to refresh the home page in milliseconds #
 (This updates jobs) #
 Default setting is '2000', updating the home page every 2 seconds.
use_icons:
Use icons, if false A.R.M will use text 
##ripping|transcoding|etc
save_remote_images:
Save job images locally ? 
# If True: A.R.M will copy remote images to a local directory and use the update the job to use the local version.
bootstrap_skin:
Bootstrap remote skin 
# You can find all versions available from 
#https://www.bootstrapcdn.com/bootswatch/
# You will need to refresh the page to see the updated bootstrap skin
language: What language do you want A.R.M to use ?
database_limit: limit the number of jobs to show on each page, over 175 will make things go funky!
notify_refresh: Set the Notification timeout value in milliseconds, values less than 1 second should be avoided.
